narrative:
Markets are in Business as Usual mode with a stress score of 16 out of 100, reflecting a genuinely calm environment. The modest 0.9% five-day gain in SPY, with a z-score of just 0.23, confirms that equity movement is well within normal bounds with no directional urgency in either direction.

The VIX sits at 16.7, placing it at the 35th percentile of its ten-year range, which is neither elevated nor suppressed but simply ordinary. The term structure remains in normal contango, supporting the baseline assumption of stable conditions. Credit spreads are confirming this equity calm decisively: high-yield OAS at 279 basis points ranks in the 12th percentile versus five-year history at a z-score of minus 0.97, while investment-grade spreads at 76 basis points are similarly tight at minus 1.07. Both credit indicators are tighter than average, which is consistent with low equity stress rather than contradicting it.

Breadth is entirely unremarkable with zero sectors trading red and zero emerging markets in negative territory. Germany has been the standout performer with EWG up 3.7% over five days, followed by utilities and the UK at plus 3.4% and plus 3.3% respectively. China's equity benchmark has lagged with FXI down 1.9%, though this represents normal relative weakness rather than a market dislocation.

Nothing is flagged as a dislocation or deep value opportunity at this time. The market environment remains balanced and stable with no tactical opportunities jumping out on either an absolute or relative basis.
